summary: Matched the NokoNoko item implementation by replacing placeholders in src/melee/it/items/itnokonoko.c with real state-table-driven item logic, typed prototypes, item variable structs, and related shell/helper declarations. The PR also flipped itnokonoko.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and adjusted symbol metadata so many internal itnokonoko functions/data became local.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itnokonoko.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Replaced placeholder stubs in itnokonoko.c with a roughly 545-line implementation including ItemStateTable it_803F85F0 and functions spanning it_802DC69C through it_802DDA84.
Added concrete itnokonoko includes for camera, ftlib, item helpers, collision, zgshell, and zrshell instead of platform/placeholder-only includes.
Added itNokoNoko_ItemVars, itNokoNoko_DatAttrs2, and itNokoNoko_DatAttrs to itCommonItems.h, and added nokonoko to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in types.h.
Moved struct ECB out of src/melee/it/types.h and into src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h, where it is used by itNokoNoko_ItemVars at x74.
Converted itnokonoko.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to typed signatures using Item_GObj*, Vec3*, bool, int, void, and Item_StateChangeFlags.
Refined related item helper prototypes in it_2725.h, including it_8027B5B0 taking a Vec3* instead of an s32 for its position argument and it_8027C794 taking Item_GObj*.
Updated it_8027C794 in it_2725.c to accept Item_GObj* while preserving its false return behavior.
Removed an unnecessary cast in itheiho.c after the it_8027B5B0 prototype was corrected: it_8027B5B0(0xD2, arg1, NULL, NULL, 1).
Typed shell-related prototypes used by itnokonoko, including it_802E0100(s32, Vec3*, s32) in itzgshell.h and it_802E0488(s32, Vec3*, s32) in itzrshell.c/.h.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark many itnokonoko functions as local rather than global, and classified several data symbols as local strings or adjusted float scope metadata.
smart_moves: The PR did not just force-match one C file; it corrected cross-file prototypes so callers could pass real Vec3* and Item_GObj* values without casts or UNK_PARAMS leakage.
Adding itNokoNoko_ItemVars to the Item union made xDD4 accesses explicit as ip->xDD4_itemVar.nokonoko fields, which improves future item decomp readability and reduces raw offset guessing.
The small coll_inline helper centralizes the repeated mpColl_800436D8 call using facing_dir-to-sign conversion, avoiding repeated expression drift across matched functions.
Symbol scope cleanup aligned the symbol map with the implementation: internal state callbacks and helper functions were made local while public entry points remained global.

The implementation reused existing item infrastructure such as ItemStateTable, Item_80268E5C, it_8026D62C, it_8027C56C, it_80272860, it_8027327C, and mpColl_800471F8 rather than introducing new abstractions.

The diff shows targeted prototype fixes in related shell files because NokoNoko can spawn or interact with green/red shell item code; this prevented local matching work from depending on incorrect external declarations.
decomp_lessons: When matching an item file, expect item-specific xDD4_itemVar struct work and update the shared Item union instead of leaving opaque field access.
Correct helper prototypes early: a wrong s32 in a shared spawn function can force casts in existing callers and obscure the real ABI for future matching.
State-table item implementations benefit from giving every callback a precise return type; bool/int/void distinctions matter for matching and readability.
If a file's functions are only used through local state tables or internal control flow, reflect that in symbols.txt as local scope after matching.
Data symbols that are actually strings should be marked with data:string and accurate sizes; this PR changed it_803F86A0, it_804D5660, and it_804D5668 accordingly.
Small PAD_STACK uses and inline helpers can be legitimate matching tactics, but they should remain close to the functions that need them and not be overgeneralized without evidence.
Moving common structs between headers can be part of matching work when a newly typed item variable struct needs the type in a different include layer.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variable structs follow the pattern itNokoNoko_ItemVars and are added as a lowercase item-name union member, here xDD4_itemVar.nokonoko.
Item data-attribute structs follow the pattern itNokoNoko_DatAttrs and itNokoNoko_DatAttrs2 when the DAT layout contains a nested pointer.
Address-based function names remain in the it_802Dxxxx range for unmatched semantic names, with fn_802DC8B8 retained for a local helper-like function.
State table symbols retain address-style names, here ItemStateTable it_803F85F0[].
Header declarations preserve address comments such as /* 2DC69C */ while replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures.
Related item shell functions keep their existing address names but gain Vec3* parameters where the call ABI indicates position vectors.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) and PAD_STACK(8) in selected functions, indicating stack-shape adjustments were needed for matching.
Introduced coll_inline(Item* ip) so repeated collision setup compiled consistently: mpColl_800436D8(&ip->x378_itemColl, (ip->facing_dir == -1.0f) ? -1 : 1).
Preserved floating constants visible in the diff, including 5.0f for it_8027C0A8 calls and 1.5707963267948966 for Y rotation setup in it_802DD7F0.
Matched state transitions through existing ItemStateTable callbacks and it_802DD4A8(gobj, state, flags), including ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and literal 0x12 flags.
Changed the shared it_8027B5B0 prototype from taking an s32 position argument to Vec3*, which removed an existing cast and likely improved register/type behavior for matching.
Adjusted symbol scope from global to local for many implementation-only functions, reducing exported-symbol pressure once the file matched.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type itNokoNoko_ItemVars fields beyond x0/x4-style offsets.
Search for follow-up work on itzgshell.c, since this PR only corrected it_802E0100's declaration while zgshell remained NonMatching in configure.py.
Search for PRs that further clean up it_2725.h helper prototypes touched here, especially it_8027C0A8, it_8027C8D0, it_8027CAD8, and it_8027CBA4.
Check whether struct ECB stayed in itCommonItems.h or was later moved to a more general collision/common header.
Search symbol history for it_803F86A0, it_804D5660, and it_804D5668 to confirm string labels or semantic names were later recovered.
